commands.review_summary:
The PR adds profile-driven Crabpot behavior evals for LCM recall and context-engine quarantine, a behavior-eval CLI/report, README docs, an npm script, and focused node:test coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a feature PR adding a new behavior-eval gate. The blocking reviewer finding is source-reproducible because current main routes default checks through `scripts/run-static-suite.mjs` while the branch only shows stale package-level check wiring.
commands.review_followup: - The branch is dirty against current main, and the remaining blockers combine a rebase/static-suite integration issue with maintainer acceptance of the live npm execution boundary and CodeQL alert.

target.title: Add Crabpot LCM behavior eval gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Extend the existing LCM behavior eval PR with a context-engine quarantine gate for broken memory/context plugins.
- Keep the LCM gate explicit about cross-session recall so Crabpot tests the actual active-slot behavior, not just API registration.
- Harden `scripts/behavior-eval.mjs` around local execution contracts, isolated state, command quoting, timeout cleanup, gateway readiness, and health assertions.

## HTML Artifact

- PageDrop: https://pagedrop.ai/g/Patrick-Erichsen/1218bd734f4535c9ae83552694097862
- Backing gist: https://gist.github.com/Patrick-Erichsen/1218bd734f4535c9ae83552694097862

## Verification

- `node --test test/behavior-eval.test.mjs` -> 20 passing tests
- `npm run eval:behavior -- --profile forward-context-engine-quarantine-gate --json` -> `planned-known-failure`, `failureClass: context-engine-quarantine-missing`
- `git diff --check`
- Autoreview: `autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- GitHub checks: all current required rows green except `Static checks (windows-latest)`, which is still running at the latest poll; Dependabot auto-merge row is skipped as expected.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Crabpot now has a behavior-eval gate that can track context-engine plugin quarantine/fallback behavior, plus the existing LCM memory-recall gate.

Real environment tested: local macOS worktree, Node test runner, Crabpot behavior eval CLI, isolated temp OpenClaw state for fixture execution planning, and GitHub Actions CI for fixture/static/code scanning rows.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: see the Verification section commands above.

Evidence after fix: the quarantine gate now reaches the expected evaluation classification instead of silently passing seam-only checks; it is intentionally marked as a known failure until the OpenClaw core health contract from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87640 ships in `openclaw@latest`.

Observed result after fix: behavior eval tests pass, the quarantine profile reports `context-engine-quarantine-missing` as a planned known failure on current latest, and the runner rejects unsafe/non-local execute modes instead of leaking host state or accepting shell-shaped inputs.

What was not tested: the quarantine scenario as a must-pass live install against a released OpenClaw version, because the required health surface is in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87640 and is not yet published as `openclaw@latest`.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
